source: Verified specifications from manufacturer data. Two variants: '59 and '65 D (Tune-O-Matic + Humbuckers). Neck dimensions, joint angles, headstock angles confirmed from spec sheet 2026-03-29.
notes: The Melody Maker was Gibson's entry-level solid body from 1959 onward. Notable for unusually shallow joint angles (1.5 deg on '59, 2.5 deg on '65) compared to other Gibson instruments. Budget line — simplified manufacturing justified the shallow neck pitch.

melody_maker_59.description: Gibson Melody Maker 1959 — single cutaway solid mahogany body, single coil pickup, slab fretboard. Gibson's affordable student guitar. Shallow 1.5 deg neck angle is the shallowest in the Gibson lineup.

neck.depth_note: Neck-only depth (back of neck to underside of fretboard). Add fretboard thickness (~5mm) for total stack.
neck.neck_angle_note: 1.5 deg is the shallowest neck angle in the Gibson spec library. Shallower than LP (4 deg), ES-335 (4 deg), Explorer (3.5 deg). Simplified assembly for student market.

melody_maker_d_65.description: Gibson Melody Maker D 1965 — double cutaway, Tune-O-Matic bridge, humbucker pickups. Mid-60s update with dual cutaway body and upgraded hardware. Unusual neck taper: thin at 1st fret (same as Explorer) but grows to thickest 12th fret in the Gibson library.

neck.depth_note: Neck-only depth (back of neck to underside of fretboard). Add fretboard thickness (~5mm) for total stack.
neck.taper_note: Unusual taper: 20.32mm at 1st (same as Explorer) growing to 25.4mm at 12th — 5.08mm growth vs Explorer's 1.27mm growth. Produces a distinctive feel that starts slim and becomes very chunky.
neck.neck_angle_note: 2.5 deg — shallower than LP/ES-335 (4 deg) and Explorer (3.5 deg). Required by Tune-O-Matic bridge height.

comparison_notes.joint_angles: Both variants have shallower joint angles than any other Gibson in the library. '59 at 1.5 deg is the shallowest Gibson angle documented.
comparison_notes.neck_taper_65: The '65 D has the most extreme neck taper in the library — starts at Explorer-thin (20.32mm) and grows to the thickest 12th fret (25.4mm) of any Gibson spec.
comparison_notes.vs_explorer: Explorer joint angle 3.5 deg vs Melody Maker '59 at 1.5 deg — 2 deg shallower. Both are Gibson set-neck instruments.
